sourceMovie.title: Parasyte: Part 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Parasyte: Part 2 (2015) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Takashi Yamazaki's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Parasyte: Part 1
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Parasyte: Part 2 clicked for you, Parasyte: Part 1 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Parasyte: Part 2?
faq.answer: Try Parasyte: Part 1, Megaboa, Attack on Titan II: End of the World—each shares DNA with Parasyte: Part 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Parasyte: Part 2?
faq.answer: Parasyte: Part 2 (2015) is best described as Science Fiction, Horror, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Parasyte: Part 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Parasyte Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
